hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:A tumbler (full of milk or water) was brought to the Prophet (ﷺ) who drank from it, while on his right side there was sitting a boy who was the youngest of those who were present and on his left side there were old men. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked, "O boy, will you allow me to give it (i.e. the rest of the drink) to the old men?" The boy said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I will not give preference to anyone over me to drink the rest of it from which you have drunk." So, the Prophet (ﷺ) gave it to him
question: What does this incident teach about the etiquette of sharing and giving preference to others?
verification_reason: The incident teaches that the boy showed humility and refused to give preference to others, which the Prophet (ﷺ) appreciated and gave the drink to him.

hadith-eng: Narrated Az-Zuhri:Anas bin Malik said, that once a domestic sheep was milked for Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) while he was in the house of Anas bin Malik. The milk was mixed with water drawn from the well in Anas's house. A tumbler of it was presented to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) who drank from it. Then Abu Bakr was sitting on his left side and a bedouin on his right side. When the Prophet (ﷺ) removed the tumbler from his mouth, `Umar was afraid that the Prophet (ﷺ) might give it to the bedouin, so he said. "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Give it to Abu Bakr who is sitting by your side." But the Prophet (ﷺ) gave it to the bedouin, who was to his right and said, "You should start with the one on your right side
question: What is the significance of starting with the person on the right side in Islamic etiquette?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of starting with the person on the right side in Islamic etiquette, as instructed by the Prophet (ﷺ)

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do not withhold the superfluous water, for that will prevent people from grazing their cattle
question: What is the significance of sharing resources like water in Islam?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the significance of sharing resources like water.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:that Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do not withhold the superfluous water in order to withhold the superfluous grass
question: What is the significance of sharing resources like water and grass in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer explains the significance of sharing resources, specifically water and grass, in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah (bin Mas`ud):The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever takes a false oath to deprive somebody of his property will meet Allah while He will be angry with him." Allah revealed: 'Verily those who purchase a little gain at the cost of Allah's covenant, and their oaths.' ........(3.77) Al-Ashath came (to the place where `Abdullah was narrating) and said, "What has Abu `Abdur- Rahman (i.e. `Abdullah) been telling you? This verse was revealed concerning me. I had a well in the land of a cousin of mine. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked me to bring witnesses (to confirm my claim). I said, 'I don't have witnesses.' He said, 'Let the defendant take an oath then.' I said, 'O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! He will take a (false) oath immediately.' Then the Prophet (ﷺ) mentioned the above narration and Allah revealed the verse to confirm what he had said." (See Hadith No)
question: What are the consequences of taking a false oath to deprive someone of their property in Islam?
verification_reason: The answer text accurately describes the consequences of taking a false oath to deprive someone of their property in Islam, as stated by the Prophet (ﷺ) and supported by a Quranic verse.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There are three persons whom Allah will not look at on the Day of Resurrection, nor will he purify them and theirs shall be a severe punishment. They are: -1. A man who possessed superfluous water on a way and he withheld it from travelers. -2. A man who gave a pledge of allegiance to a ruler and he gave it only for worldly benefits. If the ruler gives him something he gets satisfied, and if the ruler withholds something from him, he gets dissatisfied. -3. And a man who displayed his goods for sale after the `Asr prayer and he said, 'By Allah, except Whom None has the right to be worshipped, I have been given so much for my goods,' and somebody believes him (and buys them). The Prophet (ﷺ) then recited: "Verily! Those who purchase a little gain at the cost of Allah's Covenant and their oaths
question: What are the consequences for those who withhold resources from others in need according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text describes the consequences of withholding resources from others in need, specifically mentioning severe punishment.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair:An Ansari man quarreled with Az-Zubair in the presence of the Prophet (ﷺ) about the Harra Canals which were used for irrigating the date-palms. The Ansari man said to Az-Zubair, "Let the water pass' but Az-Zubair refused to do so. So, the case was brought before the Prophet (ﷺ) who said to Az-Zubair, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and then let the water pass to your neighbor." On that the Ansari got angry and said to the Prophet, "Is it because he (i.e. Zubair) is your aunt's son?" On that the color of the face of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) changed (because of anger) and he said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and then withhold the water till it reaches the walls between the pits round the trees." Zubair said, "By Allah, I think that the following verse was revealed on this occasion": "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them
question: What is the significance of seeking the Prophet's judgment in disputes according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of seeking the Prophet's judgment in disputes by providing a specific example from the Prophet's life.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Urwa:When a man from the Ansar quarreled with Az-Zubair, the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) first and then let the water flow (to the land of the others)." "On that the Ansari said, (to the Prophet), "It is because he is your aunt's son." On that the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Zubair! Irrigate till the water reaches the walls between the pits around the trees and then stop (i.e. let the water go to the other's land)." I think the following verse was revealed concerning this event: "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them
question: What is the significance of seeking the Prophet's judgment in disputes according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of seeking the Prophet's judgment in disputes by providing a specific example and referencing a relevant Quranic verse.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Urwa bin Az-Zubair:An Ansari man quarreled with Az-Zubair about a canal in the Harra which was used for irrigating date-palms. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), ordering Zubair to be moderate, said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) first and then leave the water for your neighbor." The Ansari said, "Is it because he is your aunt's son?" On that the color of the face of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) changed and he said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and withhold the water till it reaches the walls that are between the pits around the trees." So, Allah's Apostle gave Zubair his full right. Zubair said, "By Allah, the following verse was revealed in that connection": "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them." (4.65) (The sub-narrator,) Ibn Shihab said to Juraij (another sub-narrator), "The Ansar and the other people interpreted the saying of the Prophet, 'Irrigate (your land) and withhold the water till it reaches the walls between the pits around the trees,' as meaning up to the ankles
question: How does the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) demonstrate fairness and justice in resolving disputes?
verification_reason: The text demonstrates the Prophet Muhammad's (ﷺ) fairness and justice in resolving a dispute between Zubair and an Ansari man over a canal, by ordering Zubair to irrigate his land first and then withhold the water to give his neighbor a fair share.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "While a man was walking he felt thirsty and went down a well and drank water from it. On coming out of it, he saw a dog panting and eating mud because of excessive thirst. The man said, 'This (dog) is suffering from the same problem as that of mine. So he (went down the well), filled his shoe with water, caught hold of it with his teeth and climbed up and watered the dog. Allah thanked him for his (good) deed and forgave him." The people asked, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Is there a reward for us in serving (the) animals?" He replied, "Yes, there is a reward for serving any animate
question: What lesson can be learned about compassion and empathy towards animals from this story?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about compassion and empathy towards animals.

hadith-eng: Narrated Asma' bint Abi Bakr:The Prophet (ﷺ) prayed the eclipse prayer, and then said, "Hell was displayed so close that I said, 'O my Lord ! Am I going to be one of its inhabitants?"' Suddenly he saw a woman. I think he said, who was being scratched by a cat. He said, "What is wrong with her?" He was told, "She had imprisoned it (i.e. the cat) till it died of hunger
question: What lesson can be learned about the treatment of animals from the story of the woman and the cat?
verification_reason: The story illustrates the consequences of mistreating animals, as the woman was punished for imprisoning the cat.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "A woman was tortured and was put in Hell because of a cat which she had kept locked till it died of hunger." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) further said, (Allah knows better) Allah said (to the woman), 'You neither fed it nor watered when you locked it up, nor did you set it free to eat the vermin of the earth
question: What are the consequences of mistreating animals in Islam?
verification_reason: The Hadith text explicitly mentions the consequences of mistreating animals, specifically a cat, in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:Once a tumbler (full of milk or water) was brought to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) who drank from it, while on his right side there was sitting a boy who was the youngest of those who were present, and on his left side there were old men. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked, "O boy ! Do you allow me to give (the drink) to the elder people (first)?" The boy said, "I will not prefer anybody to have my share from you, O Allah's Apostle!" So, he gave it to the boy
question: What does this incident teach about the rights of individuals, regardless of age, in Islamic etiquette?
verification_reason: The incident teaches that individuals, regardless of age, have equal rights in Islamic etiquette, as demonstrated by the boy's refusal to give his share to the elder people.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my soul is, I will drive some people out from my (sacred) Fount on the Day of Resurrection as strange camels are expelled from a private trough
question: What is the significance of the Prophet's sacred Fount on the Day of Resurrection?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the significance of the Prophet's sacred Fount on the Day of Resurrection.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "May Allah be merciful to the mother of Ishmael! If she had left the water of Zamzam (fountain) as it was, (without constructing a basin for keeping the water), (or said, "If she had not taken handfuls of its water"), it would have been a flowing stream. Jurhum (an Arab tribe) came and asked her, 'May we settle at your dwelling?' She said, 'Yes, but you have no right to possess the water.' They agreed
question: What lessons can be learned from the actions of the mother of Ishmael regarding resource management and sharing?
verification_reason: The text provides a lesson on resource management and sharing, specifically the importance of considering the rights of others when utilizing shared resources.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There are three types of people whom Allah will neither talk to, nor look at, on the Day of Resurrection. (They are): -1. A man who takes an oath falsely that he has been offered for his goods so much more than what he is given, -2. a man who takes a false oath after the `Asr prayer in order to grab a Muslim's property, and -3. a man who withholds his superfluous water. Allah will say to him, "Today I will withhold My Grace from you as you withheld the superfluity of what you had not created
question: What are the consequences on the Day of Resurrection for those who take false oaths?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by describing the consequences for those who take false oaths.

hadith-eng: Narrated As-Sab bin Jath-thama:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, No Hima except for Allah and His Apostle. We have been told that Allah's Apostle made a place called An-Naqi' as Hima, and `Umar made As-Saraf and Ar-Rabadha Hima (for grazing the animals of Zakat)
question: What is the concept of Hima in Islamic tradition and who has the authority to designate it?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by explaining the concept of Hima and who has the authority to designate it.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Keeping horses may be a source of reward to some (man), a shelter to another (i.e. means of earning one's living), or a burden to a third. He to whom the horse will be a source of reward is the one who keeps it in Allah's Cause (prepare it for holy battles) and ties it by a long rope in a pasture (or a garden). He will get a reward equal to what its long rope allows it to eat in the pasture or the garden, and if that horse breaks its rope and crosses one or two hills, then all its footsteps and its dung will be counted as good deeds for its owner; and if it passes by a river and drinks from it, then that will also be regarded as a good deed for its owner even if he has had no intention of watering it then. Horses are a shelter from poverty to the second person who keeps horses for earning his living so as not to ask others, and at the same time he gives Allah's right (i.e. Zak`at) (from the wealth he earns through using them in trading etc.,) and does not overburden them. He who keeps horses just out of pride and for showing off and as a means of harming the Muslims, his horses will be a source of sins to him." When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about donkeys, he replied, "Nothing particular was revealed to me regarding them except the general unique verse which is applicable to everything: "Whoever does goodness equal to the weight of an atom (or small ant) shall see it (its reward) on the Day of Resurrection
question: What are the different intentions and outcomes associated with keeping horses according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer provides a detailed explanation of the different intentions and outcomes associated with keeping horses according to Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Khalid:A man came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and asked about Al-Luqata (a fallen thing). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Recognize its container and its tying material and then make a public announcement about it for one year and if its owner shows up, give it to him; otherwise use it as you like." The man said, "What about a lost sheep?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "It is for you, your brother or the wolf." The man said "What about a lost camel?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Why should you take it as it has got its water-container (its stomach) and its hooves and it can reach the places of water and can eat the trees till its owner finds it?
question: What steps should be taken when finding a lost item to ensure its rightful owner can reclaim it?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear and detailed procedure for handling lost items, including making a public announcement and giving it to the owner if found.

hadith-eng: Narrated Az-Zubair bin Al 'Awwam:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No doubt, one had better take a rope (and cut) and tie a bundle of wood and sell it whereby Allah will keep his face away (from Hell-fire) rather than ask others who may give him or not
question: What is the significance of earning one's livelihood through honest work in Islam?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of earning one's livelihood through honest work, as it is considered better than begging.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No doubt, you had better gather a bundle of wood and carry it on your back (and earn your living thereby) rather than ask somebody who may give you or not
question: What is the significance of self-reliance and earning one's own livelihood in Islam?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a Hadith that emphasizes the importance of self-reliance and earning one's own livelihood.

hadith-eng: Narrated Husain bin `Ali:`Ali bin Abi Talib said: "I got a she-camel as my share of the war booty on the day (of the battle) of Badr, and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave me another she-camel. I let both of them kneel at the door of one of the Ansar, intending to carry Idhkhir on them to sell it and use its price for my wedding banquet on marrying Fatima. A goldsmith from Bani Qainqa' was with me. Hamza bin `Abdul-Muttalib was in that house drinking wine and a lady singer was reciting: "O Hamza! (Kill) the (two) fat old she camels (and serve them to your guests). So Hamza took his sword and went towards the two she-camels and cut off their humps and opened their flanks and took a part of their livers." (I said to Ibn Shihab, "Did he take part of the humps?" He replied, "He cut off their humps and carried them away.") `Ali further said, "When I saw that dreadful sight, I went to the Prophet (ﷺ) and told him the news. The Prophet (ﷺ) came out in the company of Zaid bin Haritha who was with him then, and I too went with them. He went to Hamza and spoke harshly to him. Hamza looked up and said, 'Aren't you only the slaves of my forefathers?' The Prophet (ﷺ) retreated and went out. This incident happened before the prohibition of drinking
question: What were the consequences of consuming alcohol in the early Islamic period before its prohibition?
verification_reason: The text describes the consequences of consuming alcohol in the early Islamic period, specifically the harm caused to Ali's camels by Hamza's intoxication.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) decided to grant a portion of (the uncultivated land of) Bahrain to the Ansar. The Ansar said, "(We will not accept it) till you give a similar portion to our emigrant brothers (from Quraish)." He said, "(O Ansar!) You will soon see people giving preference to others, so remain patient till you meet me (on the Day of Resurrection)
question: What lesson can be learned about selflessness and generosity from the actions of the Ansar?
verification_reason: The answer text explains the selflessness and generosity of the Ansar by highlighting their willingness to share their reward with others, specifically the emigrant brothers from Quraish.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas (ra):The Prophet (ﷺ) called the Ansar so as to grant them a portion of (the land of) Bahrain. They said, "O Allah's Messenger ! If you grant this to us, write a similar document on our Quraish (emigrant) brothers." But the Prophet (ﷺ) did not have enough grants and he said: "After me you will see the people giving preference (to others), so be patient till you meet me
question: What lesson can be learned about patience when others are given preference in resources or opportunities?
verification_reason: The text teaches a lesson about patience when others are given preference, as the Prophet (ﷺ) advises the Ansar to be patient in the face of such situations.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One of the rights of a she camel is that it should be milked at a place of water
question: What are the rights of animals in Islam, particularly in relation to their care and well-being?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the care and well-being of animals, specifically camels, which is related to their rights in Islam.

hadith-eng: Narrated 'Abdullah (ra): I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say, "If somebody buys date-palms after they have been pollinated, the fruits will belong to the seller unless the buyer stipulates the contrary. If somebody buys a slave having some property, the property will belong to the seller unless the buyer stipulate that it should belong to him
question: What are the implications of buying date-palms after pollination in terms of ownership of the fruits?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the implications of buying date-palms after pollination and the ownership of the fruits.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:The Prophet (ﷺ) permitted selling the dates of the 'Araya for ready dates by estimating the amount of the former (as they are still on the trees)
question: What is the concept of 'Araya in Islamic finance and trade?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about the concept of 'Araya in Islamic finance and trade.

hadith-eng: Narrated Jabir bin `Abdullah:The Prophet (ﷺ) forbade the sales called Al-Mukhabara, Al-Muhaqala and Al-Muzabana and the selling of fruits till they are free from blights. He forbade the selling of the fruits except for money, except the 'Araya
question: What are the types of sales transactions that are prohibited in Islam according to this narration?
verification_reason: The narration specifically mentions the types of sales transactions that are prohibited, namely Al-Mukhabara, Al-Muhaqala, Al-Muzabana, and selling fruits before they are free from blights.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed the sale of the dates of the 'Araya for ready dates by estimating the former which should be estimated as less than five Awsuq or five Awsuq. (Dawud, the sub-narrator is not sure as to the right amount)
question: What is the significance of estimating the quantity in the sale of 'Araya dates?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear explanation of the significance of estimating the quantity in the sale of 'Araya dates.

hadith-eng: Narrated Rafi` 'bin Khadij and Sahl bin Abi Hathma:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the sale of Muzabana, i.e. selling of fruits for fruits, except in the case of 'Araya; he allowed the owners of 'Araya such kind of sale
question: What is the ruling on the sale of Muzabana in Islamic jurisprudence?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about the ruling on the sale of Muzabana.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Urwa:When a man from the Ansar quarreled with Az-Zubair, the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) first and then let the water flow (to the land of the others)." "On that the Ansari said, (to the Prophet), "It is because he is your aunt's son." On that the Prophet (ﷺ) said, "O Zubair! Irrigate till the water reaches the walls between the pits around the trees and then stop (i.e. let the water go to the other's land)." I think the following verse was revealed concerning this event: "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them
question: How does personal bias affect the resolution of conflicts in Islamic jurisprudence?
verification_reason: The text explains how the Prophet (ﷺ) handled a conflict between two individuals, demonstrating his impartiality and fairness, which is in line with the verse mentioned.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Urwa bin Az-Zubair:An Ansari man quarreled with Az-Zubair about a canal in the Harra which was used for irrigating date-palms. Allah's Messenger (ﷺ), ordering Zubair to be moderate, said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) first and then leave the water for your neighbor." The Ansari said, "Is it because he is your aunt's son?" On that the color of the face of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) changed and he said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and withhold the water till it reaches the walls that are between the pits around the trees." So, Allah's Apostle gave Zubair his full right. Zubair said, "By Allah, the following verse was revealed in that connection": "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them." (4.65) (The sub-narrator,) Ibn Shihab said to Juraij (another sub-narrator), "The Ansar and the other people interpreted the saying of the Prophet, 'Irrigate (your land) and withhold the water till it reaches the walls between the pits around the trees,' as meaning up to the ankles
question: What is the significance of following the Prophet's judgment in disputes according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of following the Prophet's judgment in disputes by providing a specific example where the Prophet resolves a dispute between Zubair and an Ansari man, and the Ansari man later quotes a verse from the Quran (4:65) that emphasizes the importance of the Prophet's judgment in disputes.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "While a man was walking he felt thirsty and went down a well and drank water from it. On coming out of it, he saw a dog panting and eating mud because of excessive thirst. The man said, 'This (dog) is suffering from the same problem as that of mine. So he (went down the well), filled his shoe with water, caught hold of it with his teeth and climbed up and watered the dog. Allah thanked him for his (good) deed and forgave him." The people asked, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Is there a reward for us in serving (the) animals?" He replied, "Yes, there is a reward for serving any animate
question: How does Islam view the act of kindness towards animals and what rewards are associated with it?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question about Islam's view on kindness towards animals and the associated rewards.

hadith-eng: Narrated Asma' bint Abi Bakr:The Prophet (ﷺ) prayed the eclipse prayer, and then said, "Hell was displayed so close that I said, 'O my Lord ! Am I going to be one of its inhabitants?"' Suddenly he saw a woman. I think he said, who was being scratched by a cat. He said, "What is wrong with her?" He was told, "She had imprisoned it (i.e. the cat) till it died of hunger
question: How does the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) describe his experience during the eclipse prayer in relation to Hell?
verification_reason: The answer describes the Prophet's experience during the eclipse prayer as seeing Hell and a woman being punished for her cruelty to a cat.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin `Umar:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "A woman was tortured and was put in Hell because of a cat which she had kept locked till it died of hunger." Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) further said, (Allah knows better) Allah said (to the woman), 'You neither fed it nor watered when you locked it up, nor did you set it free to eat the vermin of the earth
question: How does Islam view the responsibility of caring for animals?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a Hadith that illustrates Islam's view on the responsibility of caring for animals.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:Once a tumbler (full of milk or water) was brought to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) who drank from it, while on his right side there was sitting a boy who was the youngest of those who were present, and on his left side there were old men. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked, "O boy ! Do you allow me to give (the drink) to the elder people (first)?" The boy said, "I will not prefer anybody to have my share from you, O Allah's Apostle!" So, he gave it to the boy
question: How does this story illustrate the Prophet's (ﷺ) consideration for the feelings and rights of others?
verification_reason: The story illustrates the Prophet's consideration for the boy's feelings and rights by asking for his permission and respecting his decision.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "By Him in Whose Hands my soul is, I will drive some people out from my (sacred) Fount on the Day of Resurrection as strange camels are expelled from a private trough
question: Who might be driven away from the Prophet's Fount on the Day of Judgment?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about who will be driven away from the Prophet's Fount.

hadith-eng: Narrated Ibn `Abbas:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "May Allah be merciful to the mother of Ishmael! If she had left the water of Zamzam (fountain) as it was, (without constructing a basin for keeping the water), (or said, "If she had not taken handfuls of its water"), it would have been a flowing stream. Jurhum (an Arab tribe) came and asked her, 'May we settle at your dwelling?' She said, 'Yes, but you have no right to possess the water.' They agreed
question: How does the interaction between the mother of Ishmael and the Jurhum tribe illustrate principles of negotiation and agreement?
verification_reason: The text illustrates the mother of Ishmael's negotiation with the Jurhum tribe regarding the water of Zamzam, demonstrating principles of agreement and compromise.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "There are three types of people whom Allah will neither talk to, nor look at, on the Day of Resurrection. (They are): -1. A man who takes an oath falsely that he has been offered for his goods so much more than what he is given, -2. a man who takes a false oath after the `Asr prayer in order to grab a Muslim's property, and -3. a man who withholds his superfluous water. Allah will say to him, "Today I will withhold My Grace from you as you withheld the superfluity of what you had not created
question: Why is withholding superfluous resources from others considered a serious offense in Islam?
verification_reason: The text explains why withholding superfluous resources is considered a serious offense in Islam, as it will result in Allah withholding His Grace from the individual.

hadith-eng: Narrated As-Sab bin Jath-thama:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, No Hima except for Allah and His Apostle. We have been told that Allah's Apostle made a place called An-Naqi' as Hima, and `Umar made As-Saraf and Ar-Rabadha Hima (for grazing the animals of Zakat)
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and Caliph Umar utilize the concept of Hima for community welfare?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing examples of how the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and Caliph Umar utilized the concept of Hima for community welfare.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Keeping horses may be a source of reward to some (man), a shelter to another (i.e. means of earning one's living), or a burden to a third. He to whom the horse will be a source of reward is the one who keeps it in Allah's Cause (prepare it for holy battles) and ties it by a long rope in a pasture (or a garden). He will get a reward equal to what its long rope allows it to eat in the pasture or the garden, and if that horse breaks its rope and crosses one or two hills, then all its footsteps and its dung will be counted as good deeds for its owner; and if it passes by a river and drinks from it, then that will also be regarded as a good deed for its owner even if he has had no intention of watering it then. Horses are a shelter from poverty to the second person who keeps horses for earning his living so as not to ask others, and at the same time he gives Allah's right (i.e. Zak`at) (from the wealth he earns through using them in trading etc.,) and does not overburden them. He who keeps horses just out of pride and for showing off and as a means of harming the Muslims, his horses will be a source of sins to him." When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about donkeys, he replied, "Nothing particular was revealed to me regarding them except the general unique verse which is applicable to everything: "Whoever does goodness equal to the weight of an atom (or small ant) shall see it (its reward) on the Day of Resurrection
question: How can keeping horses be a source of reward or sin for a person?
verification_reason: The answer provides a detailed explanation of how keeping horses can be a source of reward or sin, based on the intentions and actions of the owner.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Khalid:A man came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and asked about Al-Luqata (a fallen thing). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Recognize its container and its tying material and then make a public announcement about it for one year and if its owner shows up, give it to him; otherwise use it as you like." The man said, "What about a lost sheep?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "It is for you, your brother or the wolf." The man said "What about a lost camel?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Why should you take it as it has got its water-container (its stomach) and its hooves and it can reach the places of water and can eat the trees till its owner finds it?
question: How should one handle a lost sheep according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer provides a clear and specific instruction for handling a lost sheep according to Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated Az-Zubair bin Al 'Awwam:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No doubt, one had better take a rope (and cut) and tie a bundle of wood and sell it whereby Allah will keep his face away (from Hell-fire) rather than ask others who may give him or not
question: How does self-reliance and hard work protect a person from the humiliation of begging?
verification_reason: The text explains that self-reliance and hard work (selling wood) is better than begging, thus protecting one from humiliation.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No doubt, you had better gather a bundle of wood and carry it on your back (and earn your living thereby) rather than ask somebody who may give you or not
question: How does Islam view the act of begging or asking others for help when one is capable of working?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the question of begging and the preference for self-sufficiency.

hadith-eng: Narrated Husain bin `Ali:`Ali bin Abi Talib said: "I got a she-camel as my share of the war booty on the day (of the battle) of Badr, and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave me another she-camel. I let both of them kneel at the door of one of the Ansar, intending to carry Idhkhir on them to sell it and use its price for my wedding banquet on marrying Fatima. A goldsmith from Bani Qainqa' was with me. Hamza bin `Abdul-Muttalib was in that house drinking wine and a lady singer was reciting: "O Hamza! (Kill) the (two) fat old she camels (and serve them to your guests). So Hamza took his sword and went towards the two she-camels and cut off their humps and opened their flanks and took a part of their livers." (I said to Ibn Shihab, "Did he take part of the humps?" He replied, "He cut off their humps and carried them away.") `Ali further said, "When I saw that dreadful sight, I went to the Prophet (ﷺ) and told him the news. The Prophet (ﷺ) came out in the company of Zaid bin Haritha who was with him then, and I too went with them. He went to Hamza and spoke harshly to him. Hamza looked up and said, 'Aren't you only the slaves of my forefathers?' The Prophet (ﷺ) retreated and went out. This incident happened before the prohibition of drinking
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) respond to incidents involving his family members before the prohibition of certain actions?
verification_reason: The text accurately describes an incident involving the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) and his family member Hamza before the prohibition of drinking.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) decided to grant a portion of (the uncultivated land of) Bahrain to the Ansar. The Ansar said, "(We will not accept it) till you give a similar portion to our emigrant brothers (from Quraish)." He said, "(O Ansar!) You will soon see people giving preference to others, so remain patient till you meet me (on the Day of Resurrection)
question: How does patience play a role in dealing with situations where others are given preference?
verification_reason: The answer text addresses the role of patience in dealing with situations where others are given preference.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas (ra):The Prophet (ﷺ) called the Ansar so as to grant them a portion of (the land of) Bahrain. They said, "O Allah's Messenger ! If you grant this to us, write a similar document on our Quraish (emigrant) brothers." But the Prophet (ﷺ) did not have enough grants and he said: "After me you will see the people giving preference (to others), so be patient till you meet me
question: How did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) address the concerns of the Ansar regarding the distribution of land?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the concerns of the Ansar regarding the distribution of land and the Prophet's response to it.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "One of the rights of a she camel is that it should be milked at a place of water
question: How does Islam emphasize the importance of providing for animals' needs, such as access to water?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning a specific right of a she camel related to access to water.

hadith-eng: Narrated 'Abdullah (ra): I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say, "If somebody buys date-palms after they have been pollinated, the fruits will belong to the seller unless the buyer stipulates the contrary. If somebody buys a slave having some property, the property will belong to the seller unless the buyer stipulate that it should belong to him
question: How does the ownership of a slave's property transfer during a sale according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by explaining the Islamic teachings on the transfer of ownership of a slave's property during a sale.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Thabit:The Prophet (ﷺ) permitted selling the dates of the 'Araya for ready dates by estimating the amount of the former (as they are still on the trees)
question: How does estimating the amount of dates on trees facilitate trade according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The answer directly addresses the question of how estimating the amount of dates on trees facilitates trade according to Islamic teachings.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:The Prophet (ﷺ) allowed the sale of the dates of the 'Araya for ready dates by estimating the former which should be estimated as less than five Awsuq or five Awsuq. (Dawud, the sub-narrator is not sure as to the right amount)
question: What are the conditions under which the sale of 'Araya dates is permitted?
verification_reason: The text provides a specific condition under which the sale of 'Araya dates is permitted, which is estimating the former as less than five Awsuq or five Awsuq.

hadith-eng: Narrated Rafi` 'bin Khadij and Sahl bin Abi Hathma:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) forbade the sale of Muzabana, i.e. selling of fruits for fruits, except in the case of 'Araya; he allowed the owners of 'Araya such kind of sale
question: Under what circumstances is the sale of 'Araya permitted in Islam?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by mentioning the specific exception to the prohibition of Muzabana.

hadith-eng: Narrated Sahl bin Sa`d:A tumbler (full of milk or water) was brought to the Prophet (ﷺ) who drank from it, while on his right side there was sitting a boy who was the youngest of those who were present and on his left side there were old men. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked, "O boy, will you allow me to give it (i.e. the rest of the drink) to the old men?" The boy said, "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! I will not give preference to anyone over me to drink the rest of it from which you have drunk." So, the Prophet (ﷺ) gave it to him
question: What can be learned about the importance of seeking permission and respecting the wishes of others from this event?
verification_reason: The event illustrates the importance of seeking permission and respecting the wishes of others, as the Prophet (ﷺ) asked the boy for permission before giving the drink to the old men.

hadith-eng: Narrated Az-Zuhri:Anas bin Malik said, that once a domestic sheep was milked for Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) while he was in the house of Anas bin Malik. The milk was mixed with water drawn from the well in Anas's house. A tumbler of it was presented to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) who drank from it. Then Abu Bakr was sitting on his left side and a bedouin on his right side. When the Prophet (ﷺ) removed the tumbler from his mouth, `Umar was afraid that the Prophet (ﷺ) might give it to the bedouin, so he said. "O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! Give it to Abu Bakr who is sitting by your side." But the Prophet (ﷺ) gave it to the bedouin, who was to his right and said, "You should start with the one on your right side
question: What can be learned about the Prophet's (ﷺ) interactions with companions and strangers from this incident?
verification_reason: The answer illustrates the Prophet's (ﷺ) teaching on the importance of starting with the right side, as mentioned in the incident.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "Do not withhold the superfluous water, for that will prevent people from grazing their cattle
question: What ethical considerations are involved in the distribution of natural resources in Islam?
verification_reason: The text addresses the distribution of a natural resource (water) and its impact on others, which is a relevant ethical consideration.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "No bloodmoney will be charged if somebody dies in a mine or in a well or is killed by an animal; and if somebody finds a treasure in his land he has to give one-fifth of it to the Government
question: How is the distribution of wealth from found treasures regulated in Islamic law?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question about the regulation of found treasures in Islamic law.

hadith-eng: Narrated Abu Huraira:Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) said, "There are three persons whom Allah will not look at on the Day of Resurrection, nor will he purify them and theirs shall be a severe punishment. They are: -1. A man who possessed superfluous water on a way and he withheld it from travelers. -2. A man who gave a pledge of allegiance to a ruler and he gave it only for worldly benefits. If the ruler gives him something he gets satisfied, and if the ruler withholds something from him, he gets dissatisfied. -3. And a man who displayed his goods for sale after the `Asr prayer and he said, 'By Allah, except Whom None has the right to be worshipped, I have been given so much for my goods,' and somebody believes him (and buys them). The Prophet (ﷺ) then recited: "Verily! Those who purchase a little gain at the cost of Allah's Covenant and their oaths
question: What is the significance of honesty in trade and the consequences of false oaths in Islam?
verification_reason: The text discusses the consequences of dishonesty in trade and false oaths, which is related to the significance of honesty in trade.

hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah bin Az-Zubair:An Ansari man quarreled with Az-Zubair in the presence of the Prophet (ﷺ) about the Harra Canals which were used for irrigating the date-palms. The Ansari man said to Az-Zubair, "Let the water pass' but Az-Zubair refused to do so. So, the case was brought before the Prophet (ﷺ) who said to Az-Zubair, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and then let the water pass to your neighbor." On that the Ansari got angry and said to the Prophet, "Is it because he (i.e. Zubair) is your aunt's son?" On that the color of the face of Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) changed (because of anger) and he said, "O Zubair! Irrigate (your land) and then withhold the water till it reaches the walls between the pits round the trees." Zubair said, "By Allah, I think that the following verse was revealed on this occasion": "But no, by your Lord They can have No faith Until they make you judge In all disputes between them
question: What lesson can be learned about the role of personal relationships in judicial decisions from this incident?
verification_reason: The answer illustrates the Prophet's (ﷺ) impartial judgment in a dispute involving a personal relationship, highlighting the importance of fairness in judicial decisions.

He will get a reward equal to what its long rope allows it to eat in the pasture or the garden, and if that horse breaks its rope and crosses one or two hills, then all its footsteps and its dung will be counted as good deeds for its owner; and if it passes by a river and drinks from it, then that will also be regarded as a good deed for its owner even if he has had no intention of watering it then. Horses are a shelter from poverty to the second person who keeps horses for earning his living so as not to ask others, and at the same time he gives Allah's right (i.e. Zak`at) (from the wealth he earns through using them in trading etc.,) and does not overburden them. He who keeps horses just out of pride and for showing off and as a means of harming the Muslims, his horses will be a source of sins to him." When Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) was asked about donkeys, he replied, "Nothing particular was revealed to me regarding them except the general unique verse which is applicable to everything: "Whoever does goodness equal to the weight of an atom (or small ant) shall see it (its reward) on the Day of Resurrection
question: What is the significance of intention in the actions related to keeping animals in Islam?
verification_reason: The text explains the significance of intention in keeping animals, specifically horses and donkeys, in Islam, and how it affects the owner's reward or burden.

hadith-eng: Narrated Zaid bin Khalid:A man came to Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) and asked about Al-Luqata (a fallen thing). The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Recognize its container and its tying material and then make a public announcement about it for one year and if its owner shows up, give it to him; otherwise use it as you like." The man said, "What about a lost sheep?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "It is for you, your brother or the wolf." The man said "What about a lost camel?" The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Why should you take it as it has got its water-container (its stomach) and its hooves and it can reach the places of water and can eat the trees till its owner finds it?
question: Why is it advised not to take a lost camel according to the Prophet's guidance?
verification_reason: The answer explains the reason why it's not advised to take a lost camel, which is that it can sustain itself.

hadith-eng: Narrated Az-Zubair bin Al 'Awwam:The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "No doubt, one had better take a rope (and cut) and tie a bundle of wood and sell it whereby Allah will keep his face away (from Hell-fire) rather than ask others who may give him or not
question: What are the consequences of relying on others for sustenance according to Islamic teachings?
verification_reason: The text directly addresses the consequences of relying on others for sustenance, highlighting the potential risk of not receiving help.

hadith-eng: Narrated Husain bin `Ali:`Ali bin Abi Talib said: "I got a she-camel as my share of the war booty on the day (of the battle) of Badr, and Allah's Messenger (ﷺ) gave me another she-camel. I let both of them kneel at the door of one of the Ansar, intending to carry Idhkhir on them to sell it and use its price for my wedding banquet on marrying Fatima. A goldsmith from Bani Qainqa' was with me. Hamza bin `Abdul-Muttalib was in that house drinking wine and a lady singer was reciting: "O Hamza! (Kill) the (two) fat old she camels (and serve them to your guests). So Hamza took his sword and went towards the two she-camels and cut off their humps and opened their flanks and took a part of their livers." (I said to Ibn Shihab, "Did he take part of the humps?" He replied, "He cut off their humps and carried them away.") `Ali further said, "When I saw that dreadful sight, I went to the Prophet (ﷺ) and told him the news. The Prophet (ﷺ) came out in the company of Zaid bin Haritha who was with him then, and I too went with them. He went to Hamza and spoke harshly to him. Hamza looked up and said, 'Aren't you only the slaves of my forefathers?' The Prophet (ﷺ) retreated and went out. This incident happened before the prohibition of drinking
question: What role did the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) play in reporting incidents that occurred during his lifetime?
verification_reason: The text describes the companions of the Prophet (ﷺ) reporting incidents that occurred during his lifetime, specifically the incident of Hamza's behavior with Ali's camels.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas:The Prophet (ﷺ) decided to grant a portion of (the uncultivated land of) Bahrain to the Ansar. The Ansar said, "(We will not accept it) till you give a similar portion to our emigrant brothers (from Quraish)." He said, "(O Ansar!) You will soon see people giving preference to others, so remain patient till you meet me (on the Day of Resurrection)
question: What is the significance of meeting the Prophet on the Day of Resurrection in the context of patience and preference?
verification_reason: The text directly answers the question by providing a context where the Prophet advises the Ansar to remain patient until they meet him on the Day of Resurrection.

hadith-eng: Narrated Anas (ra):The Prophet (ﷺ) called the Ansar so as to grant them a portion of (the land of) Bahrain. They said, "O Allah's Messenger ! If you grant this to us, write a similar document on our Quraish (emigrant) brothers." But the Prophet (ﷺ) did not have enough grants and he said: "After me you will see the people giving preference (to others), so be patient till you meet me
question: What guidance did the Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) provide for dealing with situations of perceived inequality or favoritism?
verification_reason: The text provides guidance on dealing with perceived inequality or favoritism by advising patience and warning of future situations where people may give preference to others.

hadith-eng: Narrated 'Abdullah (ra): I heard the Messenger of Allah (ﷺ) say, "If somebody buys date-palms after they have been pollinated, the fruits will belong to the seller unless the buyer stipulates the contrary. hadith-eng: Narrated `Abdullah (bin Mas`ud):The Prophet (ﷺ) said, "Whoever takes a false oath to deprive somebody of his property will meet Allah while He will be angry with him." Allah revealed: 'Verily those who purchase a little gain at the cost of Allah's covenant, and their oaths.' ........(3.77) Al-Ashath came (to the place where `Abdullah was narrating) and said, "What has Abu `Abdur- Rahman (i.e. `Abdullah) been telling you? This verse was revealed concerning me. I had a well in the land of a cousin of mine. The Prophet (ﷺ) asked me to bring witnesses (to confirm my claim). I said, 'I don't have witnesses.' He said, 'Let the defendant take an oath then.' I said, 'O Allah's Messenger (ﷺ)! He will take a (false) oath immediately.' Then the Prophet (ﷺ) mentioned the above narration and Allah revealed the verse to confirm what he had said." (See Hadith No)
